original: Tim Berners-Leereleases files describing his idea for the World Wide Web and debuts WWW as a publicly available service on the internet
Marcus Hooper, 12, becomes the youngest person to swim across the English Channel
NASA announces that life may have existed on Mars after examining meteorite ALH 84001, discovered in Antarctica and believed to be from Mars
Prometheus, the world's oldest tree, aged at least 4,862 years, is accidentally cut down in Nevada, USA
Supernova SN 1181 in the constellation Cassiopeia is observed by Chinese and Japanese astronomers and is visible for 185 days to February 6, 1182
Mount Tongariro, New Zealand, erupts for the first time in a century

American Gertrude Ederle (20) becomes the first woman to swim across the English Channel from Cap Gris-Nez, France, to Kingsdown, England, in 14 hours and 39 minutes, setting a record for males and females
Hank Aaronhits his 660th and 661st home runs for the Atlanta Braves, breaking Babe Ruth's MLB record for most home runs with one team
"Barbie" film passes $1 billion at the global box office; its director,Greta Gerwig, is the first solo female filmmaker to achieve this
Chinese diver Quan Hongchan successfully defends her 10 m platform gold medal at the Paris Olympics, adding to her 10 m synchro platform gold with partner Chen Yuxi
Chubby Checker performs his version of Hank Ballard's "The Twist" on "TheDick ClarkShow," starting a worldwide dance craze
Gherman S. Titov is the second Russian in space aboard Vostok 2 (17 orbits)
Muhammad Aliknocks out English boxer Brian London in round 3 at Earl's Court in London to retain his undisputed world heavyweight title
American children's program "Blue's Clues" concludes its 10-year run on Nickelodeon cable channel

Australian women's 4 × 100 m freestyle relay team ofEmma McKeon, Brittany Elmslie, Bronte Campbell, and Cate Campbell set a new world record of 3:30.65 to win gold ahead of the US and Canada at the Rio de Janeiro Olympics

Dutch super athlete Fanny Blankers-Koen wins the 200 m in 24.4 seconds at the London Olympics, becoming the first woman to claim three individual track and field gold medals at a Games, having already won the 100 m and 80 m hurdles
Jamaican 100/200m championElaine Thompson-Herahclaims her third track gold medal at the Tokyo Olympics as part of the winning Jamaican 4 x 100 m relay team
Wrestler Bruce Baumgartner becomes the first American to win in three straight Olympics, capturing the gold in the 286-pound freestyle division in Barcelona
Sri Lanka sets a world record of 952 for 6 in the first cricket Test against India in Colombo;Sanath Jayasuriyascores 340, Roshan Mahanama scores 225 in a record partnership of 576
UEFA Women's Euro Final: Dutch forward Vivianne Miedema scores 2 as the Netherlands beat Denmark 4-2 in Enschede, the Netherlands
Future Baseball Hall of Fame sluggerTed Williamsreturns to the Red Sox from military service in Korea and ends the abbreviated season with a .407 batting average
Baltimore's Jim Dwyer & Larry Sheets, and Rangers Toby Harrah hit MLB record 3 grand slams in Texas' 13-11 win
Jamaica becomes independent after 300 years of British rule
A quarter of humanity is running out of water, with 17 countries under extreme water stress, including Cape Town, Los Angeles, and Bangalore, according to a report by the World Resources Institute
First case of motion sickness in space reported
19th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-G): Challenger 8 lands at Edwards AFB
The Great Bell is cast for the Great Clock of Westminster, London (Big Ben)
Italian explorerJohn Cabotreturns to Bristol from North America (Newfoundland), becoming the first European to do so since the Vikings
US PresidentLyndon B. Johnsonsigns theVoting Rights Act, prohibiting voting discrimination against minorities
Thousands gather in Hiroshima for the 50th anniversary of the atomic bombing of the city
Beatles release their fifth UK album "Help!" from the film soundtrack
American punk rock band "TheRamones" performs for the last time at the Palace in Hollywood, California
Jim PalmerandJoe Morganare inducted into Baseball Hall of Fame, Cooperstown, NY
Philadelphia NBA franchise changes name from "Nationals" to "76ers," based on the US Declaration of Independence signed in Philadelphia in 1776
Battle of Junín:Simón Bolívar's army defeats the Spanish
Bolivia gains independence from Spain (National Day)
HMS Roebuck, captained byWilliam Dampier, lands at Shark Bay, Western Australia on the first British scientific expedition to Australia
Salazar Bridge over the Tagus, the longest suspension bridge in Europe, opens
Roberto Clemente,Warren Spahn, Billy Evans, Monte Irvin, Mickey Welch, and George "High Pockets" Kelly are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, NY

Festival for Peace concert is held at Shea Stadium, New York City, to commemorate the 25th anniversary of the Hiroshima bombing; performers includeJanis Joplin,Paul Simon,Creedence Clearwater Revival, Steppenwolf,Miles Davis, Johnny Winter,Herbie Hancock,Dionne Warwick, John Sebastian, The Rascals, and the Broadway cast of "Hair"
Boston appoints its first African American Police Commissioner William G. Gross
Super Rugby Final, Westpac Stadium Wellington: Wellington Hurricanes win their first Super Rugby title with a 20-3 victory over the Lions from Johannesburg
First NFL exhibition game in Europe: Minnesota Vikings beat St. Louis Cardinals 28-10 at London's Wembley Stadium
British auto racer Sterling Moss scores his 16th and final Formula 1 victory in the German Grand Prix at the Nürburgring
Harry Butler makes the first flight across a major body of water in Australia, delivering mail from Adelaide across Gulf St. Vincent to Minlaton on the Yorke Peninsula
Supertanker Castillo de Bellver catches fire and sinks northwest of Cape Town, South Africa
Newly established NHL franchise Nashville Predators name Barry Trotz as inaugural coach; lasts 15 seasons and 1,196 regular season games
Holy Roman Empire ends; it was described by Voltaire as "in no way holy, nor Roman, nor an empire." Emperor Francis II renounces the title, becoming instead the first Emperor of Austria
Norwich University is founded in Vermont as the first private military school in the United States
Treaty of The Hague is signed, whereby the Dutch Republic sells New Holland (Brazil) to Portugal for 63 tons of gold
Maya King Jasaw Chan K'awill I of Tikal defeats Yich'aak K'ahk' ("Claw of Fire"), his long-standing rival and king of Calakmul
The National Museum of Racing opens in Congress Park in Saratoga Springs, NY
Ella Jenkins, American folk singer-songwriter, and ukulele player (Multicultural Children's Songs), born in St. Louis, Missouri (d. 2024)[1]
Leslie Odom Jr., American stage actor and singer (Hamilton), born in Queens, New York City
Michael Peters, American choreographer and dancer ("Dreamgirls"; Michael Jackson videos "Beat It" and "Thriller"), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1994)
Soleil Moon Frye, American actress (Punky Brewster; Sabrina, the Teenage Witch - "Roxie"), and documentary film maker, born in Glendora, California
Ty Simpkins, American actor (Insidious; Jurassic World), born in New York City
Yuki Kajiura, Japanese film, anime, and video game music composer, born in Tokyo, Japan
Jon Postel, American computer scientist known as "the god of the internet", born in Altadena, California (d. 1998)
Elliott Smith [Steven Smith], American Oscar nominated singer-songwriter (Good Will Hunting - "Miss Misery"), born in Omaha, Nebraska (d. 2003)
